_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an index averaging coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice prices normalized to 100 in 2015, currently stands at 141. This represents a 41% increase since 2015, reflecting a significant upward move over the period. However, the rise has been uneven across components and relatively recent, indicating concentrated market pressures rather than broad-based trends. The index serves as a snapshot of key soft commodity price changes relevant to common breakfast items.
notes.movers: The principal contributors to the increase are coffee, up 99%, and orange juice, which has risen 34% since 2015. Wheat and sugar show smaller gains of 19% and 13%, respectively. These price changes are primarily driven by weather-related events and crop diseases such as frost and drought affecting coffee-producing regions and citrus greening impacting orange juice. Soft commodity markets tend to be thin and volatile, meaning these supply shocks can cause sharp price movements without reflecting wider inflationary pressures.
notes.why: The headline rise in the breakfast basket is largely due to a handful of weather-sensitive crops exposed to disease and adverse climate events. Given the continued vulnerability of these crops, input prices for affected commodities may remain elevated for several years. Such dynamics underscore the importance of monitoring climate and biological factors in evaluating soft commodity price trajectories.
